(Of course, due to the differences in callers, it is acceptable for these fields to be optional/undefined in certain cases.) ## Improvement Suggestions ### experimental.chat.system.transform **Parameters** : * input: `{ sessionID?: string; messageID?: string; model: Model }` * output: `{ system: string[] }` ### experimental.chat.messages.transform **Parameters** : * input: `{ sessionID?: string; messageID?: string; model: Model }`" ] }, { "url": "https://newreleases.io/project/github/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning/release/v1.0.0", "title": "Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning v1.0.0 on GitHub", "publish_date": null, "excerpts": [ "[](https://twitter.com/newreleasesio) [](https://github.com/newreleasesio) Pricing # github Opencode-DCP/ opencode-dynamic-context-pruning [v1.0.0](https://github.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning/releases/tag/v1.0.0) v1.0.0 - Major Architecture Rewrite on [GitHub](https://github.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning) latest releases: v3.1.12 , v3.1.11 , v3.1.10 ... 5 months ago ## What's Changed ### Highlights **Complete rewrite to use OpenCode's native hooks API** , removing ~1,400 lines of provider-specific fetch wrapper code. The plugin now uses `experimental.chat.messages.transform` and `experimental.chat.system.transform` hooks, making it dramatically simpler and more maintainable. ### Architecture * Removed `lib/fetch-wrapper/` directory with 6+ format adapters (Anthropic, Bedrock, Gemini, OpenAI) * Simplified state management, removed ID mapping layer * Consolidated tool cache logic * Net reduction of ~1,400 lines ### New Features * System prompt transformation hook for synthetic context injection * AI nudge system reminds LLM to use prune tool after N tool results * Support for `$OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR` config location * Prune tool enabled by default ### Bug Fixes * Skip inserting prunable tools list when empty * Properly reset nudge counter after prune tool use * Await session state load before syncing tool cache * Prevent compacted tools from appearing in prunable list ### Breaking Changes * On-idle strategy now marked as legacy and disabled by default **Full Changelog** : [v0.4.17...v1.0.0](https://github.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning/compare/v0.4.17...v1.0.0) Check out latest releases or releases around Opencode-DCP/ opencode-dynamic-context-pruning v1.0.0 Don't miss a new opencode-dynamic-context-pruning release NewReleases is sending notifications on new releases. Get notifications * About * [News](https://twitter.com/newreleasesio) * Contact * Terms of Service * Privacy Policy" ] }, { "url": "https://lzw.me/docs/opencodedocs/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning/appendix/architecture/index.html", "title": "Architecture: DCP Internal Design | opencode-dynamic-context-pruning | OpenCodeDocs", "publish_date": null, "excerpts": [ "Menu Back to top On this page # Architecture Overview: How DCP Works Internally ​ ## What You'll Learn ​ This section doesn't involve operations but helps you deeply understand DCP's internal design. After completing this, you will be able to: * Understand how DCP integrates with conversation flow through OpenCode's hook system * Master the responsibility boundaries and interaction patterns of each module * Understand the complete call chain from message reception to pruning completion * Learn the design philosophy behind state management, message processing, and pruning strategies ## Core Concept ​ DCP is a plugin based on OpenCode Plugin SDK that extends OpenCode's conversation capabilities by registering hooks and tools. The entire system revolves around the core concept of "message transform"—each time AI prepares to respond to a user, DCP scans, analyzes, and prunes the conversation history before sending the optimized context to the LLM. Why "Message Transform"? OpenCode provides the `experimental. chat.messages.transform` hook, allowing plugins to modify messages before they are sent to the LLM. This is exactly the capability DCP needs—removing redundant tool calls before the LLM sees the context. The system uses a modular design with clear responsibilities for each module: * **config** : Configuration management, supporting multi-level overrides * **state** : State management, maintaining session-level runtime state * **messages** : Message processing, executing actual pruning and injection operations * **strategies** : Pruning strategies, defining automatic cleanup rules * **commands** : Command system, providing manual control and statistics queries * **hooks** : Hook adapter, connecting OpenCode and DCP modules * **ui** : User notifications, displaying pruning results and statistics ## Module Architecture Diagram ​ mermaid ``` graph TB subgraph OpenCode["OpenCode Runtime"] H1["experimental.chat.system.transform
System prompt injection"] H2["experimental.chat.messages." ] }, { "url": "https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/25494", "title": "[FEATURE] pre_chat.messages.transform hook for image ... - GitHub", "publish_date": null, "excerpts": [ "## Navigation Menu Toggle navigation Appearance settings Search or jump to... # Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests... Appearance settings Resetting focus anomalyco / **opencode** Public * Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings * Fork 20\.4k * Star 170k # [FEATURE] pre\_chat.messages.transform hook for image-to-text stripping \#25494 New issue Copy link New issue Copy link Open Open [[FEATURE] pre\_chat.messages.transform hook for image-to-text stripping]() \#25494 Copy link Assignees jlongster [@n1flh31mur](https://github.com/n1flh31mur) ## Description [@n1flh31mur](https://github.com/n1flh31mur) [n1flh31mur](https://github.com/n1flh31mur) opened [on May 2, 2026](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/25494) Issue body actions ### Verification * I have verified this feature request has not been suggested before (see [Proposal: message.parts. before plugin hook for multimodal preflight \#24125](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/24125) , [system.transform fires after messages.transform — plugins can't coordinate system+message mutations \#19960](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/19960) — this issue proposes a unified approach combining their ideas) ### Problem Plugins cannot strip or transform messages before they reach the LLM. When a user attaches an image, the LLM errors out with `"this model does not support image input"` . The existing hooks have limitations: * `experimental.chat.messages.transform` has `input: {}` — it never receives messages ( [system.transform fires after messages.transform — plugins can't coordinate system+message mutations \#19960](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/19960) ) * [Proposal: message.parts.before plugin hook for multimodal preflight \#24125](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/24125) proposes `message.parts. before` for image handling but it is not implemented * No hook fires BEFORE the LLM is called ### Proposed Solution Add `pre_chat.messages.transform` — a hook that fires **before** the LLM, receiving the full messages array and allowing plugins to transform them: ``` "pre_chat.messages.transform" ?: ( input : { sessionID : string agent : string model : Model messages : { info : Message ; parts : Part [ ] } [ ] } , output : { messages : { info : Message ; parts : Part [ ] } [ ] } , ) => Promise < void > ``` ### Use Cases 1. **Image-to-text stripping** : Scan for `FilePart` with `image: true` , call a Vision server (Qwen2.5-VL-3B), replace with `TextPart` containing the description 2. **Vision plugin integration** : Transform messages on-the-fly so the LLM always receives text-only input 3. **Pre-inference modification** : Any plugin that needs to modify messages before the LLM ### Backward Compatibility * `experimental.chat.messages." ] }, { "url": "https://deepwiki.com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/5-opencode-plugin-api-reference", "title": "OpenCode Plugin API Reference | capyBearista/opencode-plugins | DeepWiki", "publish_date": "2026-05-04", "excerpts": [ "Loading... Index your code with Devin DeepWiki DeepWiki [capyBearista/opencode-plugins](https://github.com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins "Open repository") Index your code with Devin Edit Wiki Share Loading... Menu # OpenCode Plugin API Reference Relevant source files * [docs/opencode-system-prompt-guide.md](https://github.com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/blob/f379609e/docs/opencode-system-prompt-guide.md?plain=1) * [packages/opencode-agents-loader/package.json](https://github.com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/blob/f379609e/packages/opencode-agents-loader/package.json) * [packages/opencode-double-tap-timeline/package.json](https://github.com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/blob/f379609e/packages/opencode-double-tap-timeline/package.json) * [packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.test.ts](https://github.com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/blob/f379609e/packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.test.ts) * [packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts](https://github. com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/blob/f379609e/packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts) This page provides a technical reference for the `@opencode-ai/plugin` interface, the standard foundation for all OpenCode extensions. It covers the structure of plugin modules, available lifecycle hooks for both server and TUI environments, and the mechanics of the system prompt assembly pipeline. ## Plugin Module Structure All plugins must export a `PluginModule` as their default export [packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts 124-129](https://github.com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/blob/f379609e/packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts) This object defines the plugin's identity and its entry points for different execution environments. ### `PluginModule` Shape |Property |Type |Description | | --- | --- | --- | |`id` |`string` |A unique identifier (e.g., `capybearista.opencode-output-styles` ) [packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts 125](https://github. com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/blob/f379609e/packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts) | |`server` |`Plugin` |An async function that initializes server-side hooks [packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts 6](https://github.com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/blob/f379609e/packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts) | |`tui` |`TUIPlugin` |(Optional) An entry point for UI-specific logic and SolidJS components. | ### Execution Context When the `server` or `tui` functions are called, they receive a context object ( `ctx` ) containing project metadata: * `ctx.directory` : The current working directory [packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts 7](https://github.com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/blob/f379609e/packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts) * `ctx.worktree` : The root of the project workspace [packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts 7](https://github.com/capyBearista/opencode-plugins/blob/f379609e/packages/opencode-output-styles/src/index.ts) * `ctx." ] } ], "warnings": null, "usage": [ { "name": "sku_search", "count": 1 } ], "session_id": "ses_139a63fcaffe116uWiEy7yF5mB" }

Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the complete analysis.

Key findings:
1. caveman-opencode-plugin: MIT licensed, 71 stars, 9 commits, v0.1.5 published May 31, 2026
2. @tarquinen/opencode-dcp: AGPL-3.0, 3.3k stars, 1,349 commits, v3.1.12 published May 12, 2026
3. Both use @opencode-ai/plugin as peer dependency
4. caveman-opencode-plugin uses Bun for build, DCP uses tsup
5. Multiple caveman plugin variants exist: dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin, dxvapor/opencode-caveman, Mumme-IT/opencode-caveman
6. Plugin API: exports default function returning Hooks object with hooks like experimental.chat.system.transform, experimental.chat.messages.transform, command.execute.before, config, event, tool, experimental.session.compacting

Let me now write the comprehensive analysis.

Complete analysis. Here are the findings.

---

# OpenCode Plugin Ecosystem Analysis

## 1. caveman-opencode-plugin — Complete Breakdown

### What it is
A caveman communication mode plugin for OpenCode. Adapts [JuliusBrussee/caveman](https://github.com/JuliusBrussee/caveman) (54k+ stars) behavioral rules into an OpenCode hook-based plugin. Cuts ~75% output tokens by making AI respond in terse fragments.

**Source:** [dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin](https://github.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin) — 71 stars, 9 commits, 6 npm releases (v0.1.0 → v0.1.5)

### Repository Structure
```
src/
├── index.ts          # Plugin entry — exports cavemanPlugin
├── config.ts         # Config loader (caveman.json)
├── state.ts          # Per-session mode state (Map<sessionId, CavemanState>)
├── setup.ts          # Interactive setup wizard
├── commands/
│   ├── commit.ts     # /caveman-commit handler
│   ├── mode.ts       # /caveman (/caveman-mode) handler
│   └── review.ts     # /caveman-review handler
├── skills/
│   ├── caveman.ts    # System instruction generator (all modes + examples)
│   ├── commit.ts     # Commit message instruction
│   └── review.ts     # Code review instruction
└── __tests__/
    ├── commands.test.ts
    ├── config.test.ts
    └── state.test.ts
build.ts               # Bun build script
setup.sh               # Bash setup wizard
caveman.json.example   # Example config
```

**Source:** `src/` directory at [dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/tree/main/src](https://github.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/tree/main/src)

### Plugin API — How it registers

**Entry point** (`src/index.ts`):
```typescript
import type { Plugin, Hooks } from '@opencode-ai/plugin'

const cavemanPlugin: Plugin = async () => {
  const hooks: Hooks = {
    // Hook implementations
  }
  return hooks
}

export default cavemanPlugin
```

**Source:** [src/index.ts:1-3](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/src/index.ts)

### Hooks Used (3 hooks)

| Hook                               | Purpose                                                                                                                              |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `config`                             | Registers slash commands (`/caveman`, `/caveman-mode`, `/caveman-commit`, `/caveman-review`) in opencode config                              |
| `experimental.chat.system.transform` | Injects caveman system instruction into **every** LLM call. Reads mode from `state.ts` per sessionID. Appends to `output.system[]` array     |
| `command.execute.before`             | Intercepts commands before model sees them. Handles `/caveman <mode>`, `/caveman-commit`, `/caveman-review`. Mutates `output.parts` directly |

**Source:** [src/index.ts:10-80](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/src/index.ts)

### Dependencies
- **peerDependencies:** `@opencode-ai/plugin >=1.14.21`
- **devDependencies:** `@opencode-ai/plugin@1.14.21`, `@types/node`, `typescript@6.0.3`
- **Zero runtime dependencies** — only imports `fs`, `path`, `os`, `process` (Node stdlib)

**Source:** [package.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/package.json)

### Build/Publish
- **Build:** `bun run build.ts` (Bun.build API) + `tsc --emitDeclarationOnly`
- **Output:** ESM to `dist/` directory
- **TypeScript config:** target ES2022, module ESNext, bundler resolution
- **Published files:** `dist/` only
- **No CI/CD workflow** visible (no `.github/workflows/`)

**Source:** [build.ts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/build.ts), [tsconfig.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/tsconfig.json)

### Relationship to DCP
Mentioned in README as **companion plugin**: "For context compression, pair with @tarquinen/opencode-dcp." No code dependency. They solve different problems — caveman reduces **output** tokens, DCP reduces **input/context** tokens.

### Configuration
Two config sources, checked in order:
1. `./caveman.json` (project root)
2. `~/.config/opencode/caveman.json` (global)

Schema:
```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "defaultMode": "full",
  "features": {
    "caveman": true,
    "commit": true,
    "review": true
  }
}
```

**Source:** [src/config.ts:1-48](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/src/config.ts), [caveman.json.example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/caveman.json.example)

## 2. @tarquinen/opencode-dcp — Dynamic Context Pruning

### What it is
AGPL-3.0 plugin that optimizes token usage by pruning obsolete tool outputs from conversation context. 3.3k stars, 1,349 commits, v3.1.12. 10.5k weekly downloads. The most popular OpenCode plugin.

**Source:** [Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning](https://github.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning), [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tarquinen/opencode-dcp)

### How context compression works
Uses OpenCode's `experimental.chat.messages.transform` hook to modify messages **before** they reach the LLM. Session history is never modified — pruned content replaced with placeholders. Three mechanisms:

1. **Compress tool** — LLM-callable tool that replaces stale conversation spans with technical summaries
   - `range` mode: compress contiguous spans into summary blocks
   - `message` mode (experimental): compress individual messages
   - Nested summaries: overlapping compressions preserve info through layers
2. **Automatic cleanup** — deduplication (remove duplicate tool outputs), error purging
3. **AI nudges** — reminds model to use compress after N tool results

**Source:** [DCP index.ts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning/master/index.ts)

### Hooks Used (5 hooks)

| Hook                                 | Purpose                                                                          |
| ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `experimental.chat.system.transform`   | Inject DCP system prompt instructions                                            |
| `experimental.chat.messages.transform` | Main hook — prune, deduplicate, inject nudges, sync compression blocks           |
| `experimental.text.complete`           | Strip hallucinations from completed text                                         |
| `command.execute.before`               | Handle `/dcp` subcommands (stats, sweep, manual, compress, decompress, recompress) |
| `event`                                | Track compression timing via `message.part.updated` events                         |
| `config`                               | Register `dcp` command, add `compress` to primary_tools, sync permissions            |
| `tool`                                 | Register `compress` as a custom tool the model can call                            |

### Dependencies
- **peerDependencies:** `@opencode-ai/plugin >=1.4.3`
- **runtime deps:** `@anthropic-ai/tokenizer`, `@opencode-ai/sdk`, `jsonc-parser`
- **devDeps:** tsup, tsx, prettier, typescript

### Build
- **Build:** `tsup` + `tsc --emitDeclarationOnly` (more mature than caveman's Bun-only approach)
- **Pre-publish check:** `scripts/verify-package.mjs`

## 3. OpenCode Plugin API — Complete Reference

### Plugin Architecture
A plugin is a TypeScript/JS module that exports a default function returning a `Hooks` object. The function receives a context object.

**Source:** [opencode.ai/docs/plugins/](https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/)

### Context Object
```typescript
{ project, client, $, directory, worktree }
```
- `project` — Current project info
- `client` — OpenCode SDK client for AI interaction
- `$` — Bun shell API for executing commands
- `directory` — CWD
- `worktree` — Git worktree path

### Available Hooks

**Command Events:** `command.execute.before`, `command.executed`

**File Events:** `file.edited`, `file.watcher.updated`

**Message Events:** `message.part.removed`, `message.part.updated`, `message.removed`, `message.updated`

**Session Events:** `session.created`, `session.compacted`, `session.deleted`, `session.diff`, `session.error`, `session.idle`, `session.status`, `session.updated`

**Tool Events:** `tool.execute.before`, `tool.execute.after`

**Experimental Chat Hooks:**
- `experimental.chat.system.transform` — Modify system prompt array per LLM call. Input: `{ sessionID?, model }`, Output: `{ system: string[] }`
- `experimental.chat.messages.transform` — Modify message array before LLM sees it. Input: `{}`, Output: `{ messages: { info: Message, parts: Part[] }[] }`
- `experimental.text.complete` — Transform completed text output
- `experimental.session.compacting` — Customize compaction context

**Other:** `event` (generic event handler), `config` (modify opencode config), `tool` (register custom tools), `shell.env` (inject env vars), `tui.*` hooks

### Custom Tools
```typescript
import { type Plugin, tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"

export const MyPlugin: Plugin = async (ctx) => {
  return {
    tool: {
      mytool: tool({
        description: "Description",
        args: { foo: tool.schema.string() },
        async execute(args, context) {
          return `Result: ${args.foo}`
        },
      }),
    },
  }
}
```

### Plugin Loading
1. Global config → Project config
2. npm packages installed via Bun at startup, cached in `~/.cache/opencode/node_modules/`
3. Local files: `.opencode/plugins/` (project), `~/.config/opencode/plugins/` (global)

## 5. How to Create a Similar Plugin — Recipe

### Minimal plugin file structure
```
my-plugin/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── build.ts              # Bun.build or tsup
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          # Default export: Plugin function
│   ├── config.ts         # Optional config loader
│   ├── state.ts          # Optional runtime state
│   ├── commands/         # Command handlers
│   ├── skills/           # Prompt generators / system instructions
│   └── __tests__/
└── README.md
```

### package.json template
```json
{
  "name": "my-opencode-plugin",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "files": ["dist"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "bun run build.ts && tsc --emitDeclarationOnly",
    "dev": "opencode plugin dev"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "@opencode-ai/plugin": ">=1.14.21"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@opencode-ai/plugin": "1.14.21",
    "typescript": "^6.0.0"
  },
  "keywords": ["opencode", "plugin"],
  "license": "MIT"
}
```

### Core plugin pattern
```typescript
import type { Plugin, Hooks } from '@opencode-ai/plugin'

const myPlugin: Plugin = async (ctx) => {
  const hooks: Hooks = {
    // 1. Register commands
    config: async (opencodeConfig) => {
      opencodeConfig.command ??= {}
      opencodeConfig.command['mycommand'] = {
        template: '',
        description: 'My custom command'
      }
    },

    // 2. Inject system instructions every turn
    'experimental.chat.system.transform': async (input, output) => {
      output.system.push('My custom instruction for every LLM call')
    },

    // 3. Intercept commands before model sees them
    'command.execute.before': async (input, output) => {
      if (input.command === 'mycommand') {
        output.parts = [/* custom response parts */]
      }
    },

    // 4. Register custom tools the model can call
    tool: {
      mytool: {
        description: 'My custom tool',
        args: { param: { type: 'string' } },
        async execute(args, context) {
          return `Tool result for ${args.param}`
        }
      }
    }
  }

  return hooks
}

export default myPlugin
```

### Key patterns from the ecosystem

1. **Config registration** — Use `config` hook to register commands, skills paths, tools
2. **System prompt injection** — Use `experimental.chat.system.transform` to inject per-turn system instructions (like caveman rules)
3. **Message transformation** — Use `experimental.chat.messages.transform` to modify/reduce message context (like DCP pruning)
4. **Command interception** — Use `command.execute.before` to handle commands without LLM round-trips. Mutate `output.parts` directly
5. **Compaction survival** — Use `experimental.session.compacting` to ensure plugin state persists through `/compact`
6. **Per-session state** — Use `Map<sessionID, State>` (caveman pattern) or file-based persistence (DCP pattern via `~/.config/opencode/`)
7. **Config discovery** — Check project then global config, use sensible defaults (both plugins follow this)

### Key differences between plugins

| Aspect         | caveman-opencode-plugin      | @tarquinen/opencode-dcp                |
| -------------- | ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| Complexity     | Simple (3 hooks, ~200 lines) | Complex (5+ hooks, thousands of lines) |
| Dependencies   | 0 runtime deps               | 3 runtime deps                         |
| Build          | Bun.build                    | tsup                                   |
| Config         | caveman.json                 | dcp config in opencode.json            |
| State          | In-memory Map                | File-persisted + session sync          |
| License        | MIT                          | AGPL-3.0                               |
| Test framework | bun test                     | Node built-in test runner + tsx        |
